word_list.word: a civil question deserves a civil answer
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ sˈɪvəl kwˈɛstʃən dɪzˈɜːvz ɐ sˈɪvəl ˈænsɚ

senses.definition: used as a reminder of the importance of effective communication and the role that civility can play in resolving conflicts and promoting peaceful interactions

examples.x: Whether we're talking to our friends, family, or coworkers, we should keep in mind that a civil question deserves a civil answer, and that being kind and considerate can go a long way in fostering positive relationships.

word_list.word: a drink is shorter than a tale
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dɹˈɪŋk ɪz ʃˈɔːɹɾɚ ðˌænə tˈeɪl

senses.definition: used to encourage people to be efficient and effective in their communication in order to make the most of their time and keep their audience's attention

examples.x: The host reminded the guest that they didn't have all night, and that a drink is shorter than a tale.

word_list.word: a good tongue is a good weapon
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈʊd tˈʌŋ ɪz ɐ ɡˈʊd wˈɛpən

senses.definition: used to say that the ability to speak eloquently and effectively can be a powerful tool to achieve one's goals, influence others, or resolve conflicts

examples.x: In today's world, where communication is more important than ever, having strong verbal skills can be a valuable asset - a good tongue is a good weapon.

word_list.word: a good listener is a silent flatterer
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈʊd lˈɪsənɚɹ ɪz ɐ sˈaɪlənt flˈæɾɚɹɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that when someone listens attentively to another person, they are showing respect and appreciation for that person's thoughts and ideas

examples.x: My grandfather always had a way of making me feel heard and understood through his generous ear and absence of interruption, for he knew that a good listener is a silent flatterer through active and empathetic listening.

word_list.word: a nod is as good as a wink to a blind (horse|man)
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ nˈɑːd ɪz æz ɡˈʊd æz ɐ wˈɪŋk tʊ ɐ blˈaɪnd hˈɔːɹs mˈæn

senses.definition: used to suggest that subtle communication or hints can be just as effective as explicit communication with someone who is ignorant or oblivious to the situation

word_list.word: (a|one) picture is worth  a thousand words
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ wˈʌn pˈɪktʃɚɹ ɪz wˈɜːθ ɐ θˈaʊzənd wˈɜːdz

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of visual aids in communication, as a single picture can be more impactful and effective than a long text in conveying complex ideas or messages

examples.x: When designing a website or presentation, it's important to use visuals to break up text and convey information in a more engaging way - after all, a picture is worth a thousand words.

senses.definition: used to say that people's actions show their true intentions or beliefs more clearly than their words

examples.x: Actions speak louder than words when it comes to leadership.
Actions speak louder than words, so it's better to show you care than just say it.
He promised to help, but actions speak louder than words, and he didn't follow through.
The company made many promises, but actions speak louder than words, and they didn't deliver.
Actions speak louder than words, which is why I didn't believe all the excuses he gave me.

senses.definition: used to emphasize that when one expresses their thoughts or gives instructions, it is important to do so with certainty and assertiveness

examples.x: In leadership roles, it's important to be clear and direct in your communication in order to motivate and inspire your team - must is a king's word.

word_list.word: a gentle tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness in it breaks the spirit
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dʒˈɛntəl tˈʌŋ ɪz ɐ tɹˈiː ʌv lˈaɪf bˌʌt pɚvˈɜːsnəs ɪn ɪt bɹˈeɪks ðə spˈɪɹɪt

senses.definition: used to imply the power of language in shaping interactions, highlighting the value of using kind and gentle words to bring joy while avoiding harm caused by harsh or deceitful language

word_list.word: be swift to hear and slow to speak
word_list.pronunciation: biː swˈɪft tə hˈɪɹ ænd slˈoʊ tə spˈiːk

senses.definition: used to imply the importance of listening carefully before speaking, highlighting the value of patience and restraint in communication to avoid misunderstandings and conflicts

word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi pˈɪktʃɚ tˈɛlz hɐz ɐ stˈoːɹi

senses.definition: used to suggest that something is obvious or self-evident based on how it appears visually, as  an image can reveal important information and convey a message without the need for words

examples.x: I don't need to hear the whole story, every picture tells a story - and from the look on your face, it's clear that you're in love with him.

senses.definition: used to encourage using words thoughtfully to avoid causing emotional harm, highlighting the power of language to affect others' feelings despite not causing physical harm

examples.x: The couple had an argument, but they both tried to be mindful of their language and remember that hard words break no bones.

word_list.word: you (can|) catch more flies with honey than (with|) vinegar
word_list.pronunciation: juː kæn kˈætʃ mˈoːɹ flˈaɪz wɪð hˈʌni ðɐn wɪð vˈɪnᵻɡɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that a positive and respectful approach is more effective in achieving one's goals than a negative or hostile one

examples.x: When trying to get my point across, I remind myself that you catch more flies with honey than vinegar, and try to be as pleasant as possible.

word_list.word: kind words will unlock an iron door
word_list.pronunciation: kˈaɪnd wˈɜːdz wɪl ʌnlˈɑːk ɐn ˈaɪɚn dˈoːɹ

senses.definition: used to suggest that using gentle language can help overcome difficulties and achieve goals as it can inspire trust in others and make them more receptive to suggestions and requests
used to suggest that something that continues for too long becomes tedious, boring, or irritating, causing people to lose interest or patience with it

examples.x: I know you're passionate about the topic, but remember that length begets loathing, so try to keep your presentation concise.

word_list.word: it is good to make a bridge of gold to a flying enemy
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ɡˈʊd tə mˌeɪk ɐ bɹˈɪdʒ ʌv ɡˈoʊld tʊ ɐ flˈaɪɪŋ ˈɛnəmi

senses.definition: used to advise one to use kindness and generosity to build positive relationships with potential enemies, rather than being confrontational or hostile

word_list.word: many a true word is spoken in jest
word_list.pronunciation: mˈɛni ɐ tɹˈuː wˈɜːd ɪz spˈoʊkən ɪn dʒˈɛst

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of paying attention to humor and joking, recognizing that they can sometimes reveal deeper truths or insights into a person's beliefs or attitudes

examples.x: At first, I thought my colleague was joking when he criticized the company's management, but later I realized that many a true word is spoken in jest.
